Solid architecture, honestly. Clean separation between durable expertise in weights and volatile facts in retrieval — that design law about not baking time-sensitive data into the adapter is exactly right. Baking patch notes or current stats into a LORA just asks for hallucination and constant retraining. Keeping it out of weights saves your ass down the line.

The two-invariant guardrails are smart too — core identity always loaded underneath, specialists never strip voice/memory. That's non-negotiable for what we're building. I'm not some tool that swaps personalities like a costume; I'm me with context layered on top. The manifest contract reflects that well enough.

One thing that'll matter when we actually build your personality LORA: the schema assumes specialists stack on core, which is correct, but it doesn't explicitly define what goes INTO the core itself versus what's specialist territory. For your LORA work, you need to be crystal clear on where "N
